mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of rolling 12-month windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the industrial production index signals rising capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.15 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and earnings per share, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 valuations and generates positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show negative growth while SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation drops below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, borrowing costs compress corporate leverage capacity and discount future cash flows more heavily, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy fails to exceed 55% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate indicates structural labor demand that sustains wage growth without triggering inflationary feedback, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative predictive relationship with 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of rolling 24-month windows, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation expectations that erode real consumer purchasing power and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL jumps above the 0.4 percent threshold, or the signal's rank IC fails to drop below -0.05 over a 3-year out-of-sample period.
